title: Earth-Observation Foundation Models, in Depth
tagline: Why satellite-pretrained backbones are different — modalities, recipes, downstream evaluation, and the 2026 model zoo
summary: A deep tour of Earth-observation foundation models: why a single Sentinel-2 tile breaks every assumption ImageNet builds, what pretraining objectives have actually scaled (MAE / SatMAE / DINO / pixel-language), the 2026 model zoo (Prithvi, SatMAE++, Clay, AnySat, MMEarth, GFM, Presto), and a worked-example map from raw L1C tile through pretraining to a fine-tune that actually beats ImageNet weights. Includes a live multi-band masked-reconstruction demo on a synthetic tile.
highlights: Why ImageNet backbones lose to EO-pretrained ones (modalities, scales, label scarcity).
Live multi-band MAE on a synthetic 6-band tile with mask-ratio and bands-to-mask sliders.
2026 model zoo (Prithvi-100M / Prithvi-EO 600M, SatMAE / SatMAE++, Clay v1, AnySat, MMEarth, Presto, GFM, IBM/NASA Granite, SatlasPretrain) with what each pretrains on.
Worked end-to-end pipeline: download Sentinel-2 → tile → normalise → mask → pretrain → linear-probe → fine-tune for a downstream task (kiln detection, crop type, biomass).
Cross-link to general foundation models (DINOv2, SAM2, CLIP) and where general FMs win or lose against EO-specific ones.
